preamble: The reduction of the voiced labiodental fricative [v] into the open central vowel [a] (or a similar vocalic reflex) is a rare form of vocalization, typically occurring in the context of extreme lenition or as a result of historical vowel-consonant mergers in syllable-final positions.

examples.note: Similar to r-vocalization, some labial fricatives in coda positions undergo weakening to vocalic off-glides or low central vowels.
Extreme lenition where the labiodental fricative is absorbed into the following nasal or reduced to a vague vocalic resonance.

sources: Danish dialectology studies on coda weakening.
